title: Tapping for Joy That Softens the Sleep System
subtitle: A tapping practice for letting small pleasures become part of how you sleep better
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 24 of a 30-day sleep program. The script addresses the survival-mode habit of getting through the day without pleasure, and reframes toward the physiological reality: joy and ease during the day make the nervous system more available for rest at night.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 24 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use during the day when you have been in survival mode — getting through without pleasure — and want to bring small joy back into the day as a route to easier sleep.

content.text: A five-minute daytime tapping practice from Day 24 of a 30-day sleep program. The script targets the habit of getting through the day without allowing pleasure or ease, and reframes toward a direct connection between small daytime joys and the nervous system's availability for sleep at night.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I forget to let myself feel good during the day, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I've been focused on getting through, not enjoying, I honour that I've been in survival mode.
Even though joy feels like a luxury, I'm open to remembering that it's fuel.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I don't have to earn pleasure — it's part of being alive.
Side of eye: My nervous system heals faster when I smile.
Under eye: I can take five minutes to enjoy a cup of tea without guilt.
Under nose: I can let beauty slow me down.
Chin: Small joys keep me connected to myself.
Collarbone: Ease is a skill I'm learning on purpose.
Under arm: Joy rewires me faster than force.
Top of head: I invite lightness into my day.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I let pleasure be part of my rest plan.
Side of eye: Joy steadies my whole body.
Under eye: I relax faster when I laugh.
Under nose: Ease is medicine.
Chin: I let life feel softer on purpose.
Collarbone: Happiness builds safety inside me.
Under arm: My body responds to joy with calm.
Top of head: I let delight reset my nervous system.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 24 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
